get.description: Return headline counts and year span for the entire corpus.

Return the distinct values feeding every dropdown in the playground.

Subject areas, publishers (with journal counts), Field of Research
codes (with human descriptions) and conference ranks. Results are
sorted in a sensible reading order so the client can use them
directly without re-sorting.

Return a paginated list of journals, optionally filtered.

All free-text filters (``search_text``, ``publisher``,
``best_subject_area``) are case-insensitive substring matches.
``ranked_only=true`` hides rows without a quartile;
``has_publisher`` and ``has_subject_area`` hide rows whose respective
column is NULL. Sortable columns: ``title``, ``publisher``,
``best_quartile``, ``best_subject_area``, ``sjr_index``.
Return the aggregated profile of one journal, optionally year-bounded.
Return full details of one journal article including its authors.

Return the per-year publication statistics that drive the line chart.

Trims a trailing partial year by default; pass
``include_partial_last_year=true`` to receive every row from the
underlying view.

schema.title: Response Get Journal Yearly Statistics Api Journals  Journal Id  Yearly Statistics Get

get.description:
Return a paginated list of articles published in the journal.

Sortable columns: ``title``, ``year``, ``pages``.

Return a paginated list of conferences, optionally filtered.

``search_text`` is a substring match against both title and acronym.
``primary_for`` is a substring match. ``ranked_only`` hides rows
without an iCore rank; ``has_acronym`` hides rows without an
acronym; ``has_for`` hides rows without a Field of Research code.
Sortable columns: ``title``, ``acronym``, ``rank_value``,
``primary_for``.
Return the aggregated profile of one conference, optionally year-bounded.
Return full details of one conference paper including its authors.

Return the per-year publication statistics that drive the line chart.

Trims a trailing partial year by default.

schema.title: Response Get Conference Yearly Statistics Api Conferences  Conference Id  Yearly Statistics Get

get.description:
Return a paginated list of articles presented at the conference.

Sortable columns: ``title``, ``year``, ``pages``.

Return the year summary timeseries optionally bounded by a range.

The last year of the corpus is sometimes a partial export (DBLP
snapshots taken mid-year). It is trimmed by default; pass
``include_partial_last_year=true`` to receive every row.

schema.title: Response List Year Summaries Api Years Get

get.description: Return the aggregate summary for one publication year.

Return journals that published in the requested year.

Sortable columns: ``title``, ``publisher``, ``best_quartile``,
``sjr_index``, ``articles_in_year``.

Return every article published in the year, optionally filtered.

Sortable columns: ``title``, ``venue_title``, ``venue_type``.

Return conferences that held papers in the requested year.

Sortable columns: ``title``, ``acronym``, ``rank_value``,
``articles_in_year``.

Return a paginated author listing, optionally filtered by name substring.

By default, only authors with at least one indexed contribution are
returned. Pass ``min_articles=0`` to include silent authors (i.e.
names present in ``lookup_authors`` but never matched to any article).

Sortable columns: ``author_name``, ``author_id``, ``total_articles``,
``earliest_year``, ``latest_year``.
Return the aggregated profile of one author.

Return the per-year article counts for the author's line chart.

Trims a trailing partial year by default.

schema.title: Response Get Author Yearly Statistics Api Authors  Author Id  Yearly Statistics Get

get.description:
Return a paginated list of every article the author has co-authored.

Sortable columns: ``title``, ``year``, ``venue_title``, ``venue_type``.
Return all (publisher, quartile, count) triples for the bar chart.

schema.title: Response Get Publisher Quartile Distribution Api Charts Publisher Quartile Distribution Get

get.description:
Return per-bucket journal counts per Kaggle BestSubjectArea.

``subject_areas`` accepts zero or more area names; omitting the
parameter returns every area. ``granularity`` buckets the X axis:
``year`` (no bucketing), ``five_year`` (2015, 2020…), ``decade``
(1990, 2000, 2010, 2020…). Trims a trailing partial year per area
by default.

schema.title: Response Get Subject Area Yearly Summary Api Charts Subject Area Yearly Summary Get

get.summary: Get Field Of Research Yearly Summary
get.description:
Return per-bucket conference counts per iCore Field of Research.

``primary_fors`` accepts zero or more codes; omitting the parameter
returns every code. Granularity behaviour mirrors the subject-area
summary endpoint.

schema.title: Response Get Field Of Research Yearly Summary Api Charts Field Of Research Yearly Summary Get

get.description: Return per-bucket article counts for each selected venue.

schema.title: Response Get Venue Comparison Api Charts Venue Comparison Get

get.description: Return aggregate metrics for each selected venue (bar chart).

schema.title: Response Get Venue Metrics Api Charts Venue Metrics Get

get.description:
Return per-venue scatter points pairing avg articles/yr with avg authors/article.

Accepts ``minimum_articles`` to discard sparse venues and
``rank_or_quartile`` to restrict the plot to a single ranking band.

schema.title: Response Get Authors Vs Articles Scatter Api Charts Authors Vs Articles Scatter Get

get.description:
Return one numeric metric tuple per journal for scatter plotting.

All free-text filters are substring matches; the response includes
``publisher``, ``best_quartile`` and ``best_subject_area`` so the UI
can colour points by any of those categorical dimensions.

schema.title: Response Get Journal Metrics Api Charts Journal Metrics Get

get.description: Liveness probe for orchestration and uptime monitors.

AuthorArticle.title: AuthorArticle
AuthorArticle.description: Single article authored by the user, regardless of venue type.

AuthorProfile.title: AuthorProfile
AuthorProfile.description: Full profile aggregating an author's publication record.

AuthorSummary.title: AuthorSummary
AuthorSummary.description:
Lightweight projection used by search and list endpoints.

Includes aggregate counts so the browse table can rank authors by
output without the caller having to follow a profile link.

AuthorYearlyStatistic.title: AuthorYearlyStatistic
AuthorYearlyStatistic.description: One bucket of an author's per-year publication count.

AuthorsVsArticlesPoint.title: AuthorsVsArticlesPoint
AuthorsVsArticlesPoint.description: One scatter-plot point per venue, articles per year vs authors per article.

ConferenceArticle.title: ConferenceArticle
ConferenceArticle.description: Single article presented at a conference.

ConferenceProfile.title: ConferenceProfile
ConferenceProfile.description: Full profile combining iCore ranking columns with aggregate stats.

ConferenceSummary.title: ConferenceSummary
ConferenceSummary.description: Lightweight projection used by list endpoints.

ConferenceYearlyStatistic.title: ConferenceYearlyStatistic
ConferenceYearlyStatistic.description: One bucket of the conference's per-year aggregate statistics.

CorpusTotalsResponse.title: CorpusTotalsResponse
CorpusTotalsResponse.description:
Top-line counts for the entire integrated dataset.

The eight values come from the single-row ``view_corpus_totals`` view
and feed the headline KPI tiles on the landing and dashboard pages.
``earliest_year`` and ``latest_year`` describe the temporal span of
articles whose year is known and non-null.

FieldOfResearchOption.title: FieldOfResearchOption
FieldOfResearchOption.description: One FoR code with its human-readable description.

FieldOfResearchYearlyEntry.title: FieldOfResearchYearlyEntry
FieldOfResearchYearlyEntry.description: Row for the Field of Research line chart.

FilterOptionsResponse.title: FilterOptionsResponse
FilterOptionsResponse.description: Distinct values feeding every dropdown in the playground sidebar.

JournalArticle.title: JournalArticle
JournalArticle.description: Single article published in a journal.

JournalMetricPoint.title: JournalMetricPoint
JournalMetricPoint.description: Single point in the journal-metrics scatter plot.

JournalProfile.title: JournalProfile
JournalProfile.description: Full profile combining ranking metrics and aggregate statistics.

JournalSummary.title: JournalSummary
JournalSummary.description: Lightweight projection used by list endpoints.

JournalYearlyStatistic.title: JournalYearlyStatistic
JournalYearlyStatistic.description: One bucket of the journal's per-year aggregate statistics.

PaperAuthor.title: PaperAuthor
PaperAuthor.description: Author entry inside a paper detail response.

PaperDetails.title: PaperDetails
PaperDetails.description: Full information about a single article including its authors.

PublisherOption.title: PublisherOption
PublisherOption.description: One publisher with the count of journals it owns.

PublisherQuartileEntry.title: PublisherQuartileEntry
PublisherQuartileEntry.description: Row for the publisher bar chart.

SubjectAreaYearlyEntry.title: SubjectAreaYearlyEntry
SubjectAreaYearlyEntry.description: Row for the BestSubjectArea line chart.

VenueComparisonPoint.title: VenueComparisonPoint
VenueComparisonPoint.description: Single (venue, year) row inside the multi-venue line chart payload.

VenueMetricsPoint.title: VenueMetricsPoint
VenueMetricsPoint.description:
Per-venue aggregates feeding the multi-venue bar chart.

Matches the brief's bar chart spec: total published articles, average
articles per year, and average distinct authors per year.

YearArticle.title: YearArticle
YearArticle.description: Article published in a given year, regardless of venue type.

YearConferenceEntry.title: YearConferenceEntry
YearConferenceEntry.description: Conference that held at least one paper in the requested year.

YearJournalEntry.title: YearJournalEntry
YearJournalEntry.description: Journal that published at least one article in the requested year.

YearSummary.title: YearSummary
YearSummary.description: Aggregate counts for a single publication year.
